app.name: Driven
app.tagline: One-way local-folder backup to Google Drive.

step1.title: Welcome to Driven
step1.body: Driven backs up your local folders to Google Drive - one way, fast, with optional encryption, scheduling, and battery awareness.

step2.title: Connect your Google account
step2.body: Driven uses your own Google OAuth credentials so your data never passes through anyone else's servers.
step2.awaitingCallback: Waiting for you to finish signing in...

guide.summary: How do I get a Client ID and secret? (about 5 minutes)
guide.intro: Driven never sees your Google password, and your files never pass through anyone else's servers. You create a free OAuth client in your own Google Cloud account and paste its ID and secret below. Here is exactly how:
guide.step1: Open the Google Cloud Console at console.cloud.google.com and sign in. Click the project menu in the top bar, click "New Project", give it a name (for example "Driven Backup"), click "Create", then select the new project.
guide.step2: In the left menu, open "APIs & Services", then "Library". Search for "Google Drive API", open it, and click "Enable".
guide.step3: Open "Google Auth Platform", then "Branding", and click "Get started". Enter an "App name", pick your address for "User support email", then click "Next".
guide.step4: For "Audience", choose "External" and click "Next". Enter your email under "Contact Information", click "Next", agree to the policy, then click "Create".
guide.step5: Open the "Audience" page. Under "Test users", click "Add users", enter your own Google email, and click "Save". Only test users can sign in until the app is published.
guide.step6: Open "Google Auth Platform", then "Clients" (or "APIs & Services" > "Credentials" > "Create credentials" > "OAuth client ID"). Click "Create client", set "Application type" to "Desktop app", then click "Create".
guide.step7: Copy the "Client ID" and "Client secret" shown in the dialog and paste them into the fields below. The secret is only shown once, so copy it now.
guide.testingCaveat: Note: while your project stays in the "Testing" publishing status, Google expires the sign-in after about 7 days, so you will occasionally have to sign in again. To make it permanent, click "Publish app" on the "Audience" page once everything works.

manualOpen.prompt: Your browser should have opened a Google sign-in page. If it did not open:

step3.title: Pick your first backup source
step3.body: Choose a local folder to back up and the Google Drive destination it syncs to.

step4.title: Encryption
step4.body: Turn on end-to-end encryption for this source. Driven encrypts file contents and names before upload; only you can read them.
step4.recoveryWarning: Save your recovery phrase. If you lose it and your device, your backup cannot be decrypted.

step5.title: Start your first sync
step5.body: Driven is ready. Your first backup will start now and continue in the background.

accounts.title: Connected accounts
accounts.deleteRemoteLabel: Also delete backed-up files from Drive

sources.title: Backup sources
sources.deleteRemoteLabel: Also delete backed-up files from Drive
sources.pendingRecoveryAckTooltip: Reveal and save this source's recovery phrase before it can be enabled.
sources.revealAckIntro: Reveal this source's recovery phrase, save it somewhere safe, then confirm to start its encrypted backups.
sources.pendingRemoveWarning: This source has not saved its recovery phrase yet. Removing it discards the encrypted setup, including the account's master key when no other encrypted source needs it.

preview.truncated: Showing a sample; more files match.

rules.title: Global rules
rules.telemetryNote: Anonymous usage stats only - no file names or contents are ever sent. One click to disable.

hooks.title: Backup hooks
hooks.note: Commands run in your shell once per backup cycle. A non-zero exit from the before-hook skips that backup. DRIVEN_HOOK, DRIVEN_ACCOUNT_ID, and (for the after-hook) DRIVEN_RESULT are set.

schedule.label: Only back up during a schedule window
schedule.note: Outside this window backups pause and resume automatically. Times are in this computer's local time zone.

about.title: About Driven
about.installUpdateMacUnsupported: In-app updates are not available on macOS. Download the latest version manually:

changelog.title: What's new in {version}
changelog.noNotes: No release notes were provided for this version.

recoveryPhrase.title: Your recovery phrase
recoveryPhrase.instructions: Write down these 24 words in order and keep them somewhere safe. They are the only way to recover an encrypted backup if you lose this device.
recoveryPhrase.confirmedLabel: I have saved my recovery phrase
recoveryPhrase.revealFirstHint: Reveal your recovery phrase before confirming you have saved it.

activity.title: Activity
activity.subtitle: Live feed and history of every backup event.
activity.empty: No activity yet. Events appear here as Driven backs up your files.
activity.allLoaded: You have reached the start of the activity log.

restore.title: Restore

empty.tree: No backed-up files in this folder yet.

restore.truncated: Showing the first {count} items in this folder. Use search to find more.

browser_open_failed.long: Driven could not open your browser for Google sign-in. Use the "Open the sign-in page" button below, or copy the link into your browser to continue.

invalid_grant.long: Your Google sign-in expired or was revoked. Reconnect the account to continue backing up.

consent_required.long: Google needs you to grant access. Complete the sign-in to continue.

network_unreachable.long: Driven could not reach accounts.google.com. Check your internet connection and try again.

rate_limited.long: Google Drive asked Driven to slow down. Driven is backing off and will retry automatically.

daily_quota_exhausted.long: Drive's daily request limit was reached. Driven will resume after the limit resets.

quota_exhausted.long: Your Google Drive storage is full. Free up space or upgrade your plan to continue backing up.

upload_size_limit.long: This file exceeds Google Drive's maximum file size and cannot be uploaded.

checksum_mismatch.long: The uploaded file did not match its checksum. Driven will re-upload it.

unreachable.long: Google Drive is unreachable right now. Driven will keep retrying.

resumable_session_invalid.long: A resumable upload session expired. Driven will restart the upload.

dest_folder_missing.long: The Drive destination folder was deleted. Pick a new destination to resume backups.

dest_folder_permission_denied.long: The Drive destination folder is no longer writable by this account. Check its sharing settings.

file_locked.long: A file could not be read because another program has it locked. Driven will retry later.

vss_unavailable.long: Backing up locked files needs Volume Shadow Copy, which requires running Driven elevated.

file_changed_during_upload.long: A file changed while it was being uploaded. Driven re-queued it for the next cycle.

file_replaced_during_upload.long: A file was replaced while it was being uploaded. Driven re-queued it for the next cycle.

io_error.long: Driven hit a disk error reading a file. Check the drive and try again.

path_too_long.long: A file path exceeds the operating system's length limit and was skipped.

unicode_collision.long: Two files have names that normalize to the same text. Rename one to back both up.

disk_full.long: The disk ran out of space during a read or restore. Free up space and try again.

invalid_filename.long: A file has a name Google Drive will not accept (a reserved name or trailing dot/space) and was skipped.

ads_skipped.long: An NTFS alternate data stream was skipped; the file's main contents were backed up.

offline.long: Your device reports no network connection. Backups will resume when you are back online.

no_internet.long: You are connected to a network but the internet is unreachable.

dns_failed.long: Driven could not resolve a known-good domain. Check your DNS settings.

captive_portal.long: A captive portal is blocking access. Sign in to the network and try again.

timeout.long: A request took too long and timed out. Driven will retry.

intermittent.long: The connection is unstable. Driven paused requests and will retry shortly.

proxy_required.long: An HTTP proxy requires authentication. Update your proxy settings.

endpoint_unreachable.long: Driven could not reach the update server. Try again later.

signature_invalid.long: The update's signature could not be verified and was rejected for your safety.

manual_required_macos.long: Automatic updates are not available on macOS. Download and reinstall the latest release to update Driven.

key_missing.long: The encryption key for this source was not found in the keychain.

decrypt_failed.long: A file could not be decrypted. The data may be corrupt or the key is wrong.

recovery_phrase_invalid.long: That recovery phrase failed its checksum. Check the words and their order.

db_locked.long: Driven's database is briefly locked. This usually clears on its own.

db_corrupt.long: Driven's database failed an integrity check. Rebuilding from your Drive backup is advised.

reconcile_orphan.long: Driven found a remote file with no local record and reconciled it during startup.

timeout.long: A stress-test scenario exceeded its time budget.

bug.long: Driven hit an unexpected error. Please export a diagnostic bundle and report it.

invalid_input.long: One of the values you entered is not valid. Check the highlighted field and try again.
